prompt: A vertical Chinese ink-wash tourism poster on an off-white textured rice-paper background, with a large flowing red silk ribbon that rises in an S-curve from the lower right to the upper left and transforms into drifting mist, visually connecting 5 scenic vignettes. At the bottom right, place a lone ancient Chinese figure in long white Hanfu with a high topknot, seen from behind, one arm raised as if guiding or releasing the ribbon; the robe has subtle red sash accents that echo the ribbon. Build the composition upward through layered pale gray mountains, clouds, mist, and sparse birds, in an elegant shanshui painting style mixed with refined travel-poster design. Include 5 distinct labeled scenic areas embedded along the ribbon path: 1) a towering central mountain peak emerging from clouds with pine trees, labeled vertically in Chinese near the top; 2) a traditional waterside old town with white walls, gray tile roofs, calm reflective water, and a small stone arch bridge, labeled vertically; 3) a stone-built cliff village clustered on rugged slopes beside a waterfall and stream, labeled vertically; 4) a modern glass building perched dramatically on a rocky mountaintop, labeled vertically; 5) a wide flower field or pastoral plain with pink and white blossoms, a windmill, distant mountains, and winding paths, labeled vertically. Use soft ink tones, muted grays, warm beige rock, and restrained dusty red as the main accent color. Leave generous negative space. Add large vertical brush-calligraphy headline text on the lower left reading 黄帝缙云 人间仙都, with smaller supporting Chinese calligraphy seals and printed location text near the bottom left reading 浙江·缙云 / ZHEJIANG JINYUN. Include multiple red artist seals and elegant vertical scenic labels in traditional poster layout. The overall mood is poetic, airy, cultural, premium, and serene, like a high-end destination branding poster for Jinyun.
